what_it_does: This file is a descriptive design map of the Company runtime spine (services, bridge, session supervisor, CLI, systemd, shared Suite) and a proposal for fusing OpenWebUI as an additional HTTP face that consumes the same brain, memory, and tools rather than replacing them.
observations: Maintains a YAML-frontmatter coverage block listing files read and a last-read date of 2026-06-28.
Catalogues ~25 managed services in ops/services.json with id, port, group, manage.type, systemd unit, health endpoint, vram_mb, autostart flag, and config.
Describes the Bridge on localhost:8770 as the operator UI/API with a single shared Suite binding and ~100 GET/POST routes.
Describes the Session Supervisor on 127.0.0.1:8771 as the sole owner of live Claude Code subprocesses with spawn/inject/interrupt/teardown endpoints and a 3-session concurrency cap.
States that nothing auto-starts at boot except reach services (ollama, tailscale); everything else is on-demand via the company CLI.
Notes the operator-token fail-closed gate for consequential actions and tool invocation.
Includes a data-flow diagram showing OpenWebUI talking to the Bridge, which then uses the shared Suite and the read-only Session Supervisor.
Breaks integration into four phases from understanding the API to full fusion and polish.
Lists flagged questions about replacement vs. augmentation, model selection, long-context handling, token inheritance, and canvas coexistence.
standouts: The shared Suite is intentionally singular: bridge and MCP face use the same brain, embedder, memory, and graph.
OpenWebUI is positioned as a third HTTP face on the bridge, not a separate backend or supervisor.
The Session Supervisor is the only process that spawns or owns live Claude Code sessions.
Consequential bridge routes are fail-closed behind an operator token gate.
Services are on-demand by policy, with GPU budgeting enforced by the company CLI.
conventions: Uses map/descriptive YAML frontmatter with coverage metadata.
Presents route lists, systemd unit tables, and CLI command tables as structured documentation.
Labels design decisions as issues with proposed solutions and explicit questions.
References specific file paths and approximate line ranges for traceability.
concerns: The last_read date and some live-status claims are future-dated relative to current knowledge.
Route counts are approximate ('~100', '40+', '30+') and not verifiable from this file alone.
OpenWebUI's actual backend API contract is assumed but not specified.
Auth/token propagation for a separate OpenWebUI server is proposed but unresolved.
Some Phase 2/3 recommendations presuppose implementation choices before Phase 1 questions are answered.
notes: A high-level fusion map that says OpenWebUI should plug into the existing bridge as a UI consumer of the shared Suite, leaving the supervisor and service lifecycle untouched.
questions: What exact backend API does OpenWebUI expect from a chat server?
Will OpenWebUI replace the vite canvas, augment it, or serve a different user persona?
How would an operator token or attribution flow to a separate OpenWebUI Node server?
Which model-selection policy will be adopted: implicit auto-load, explicit pre-load, or consent-gated?
Will OpenWebUI chat sessions be fresh per conversation or feed into the bridge's long-context RHM history?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches content: it is a descriptive map of live infrastructure plus an integration design ready for review.
apparent_themes: Single source of truth for services and state
On-demand resource governance with GPU ceilings
Operator attribution and consent gating for consequential actions
Additive integration that avoids duplicating the brain or session fabric
Shared cognition substrate across multiple HTTP faces
intention_signals: The file is trying to show how OpenWebUI can be grafted onto the existing Company agent OS without spawning a second Suite, second supervisor, or separate model backend, while preserving safety and shared memory.
novelty: It treats OpenWebUI not as a self-hosted LLM server but as a pure UI frontend that routes chat, model selection, voice, and tools through the existing bridge and shared Suite.
contribution: Provides a neutral integration sketch that identifies the exact seams to reuse (bridge routes, shared Suite, supervisor read surface, CLI resource manager) and the unresolved design decisions that must be settled before implementation.
summary_for_embedding: A descriptive design map of the Company agent OS spine and a proposal to integrate OpenWebUI as an additive HTTP face consuming the bridge's shared Suite, tools, and voice loop without duplicating brain or session ownership.
intention_for_embedding: To plan an OpenWebUI integration that reuses existing runtime surfaces and governance rather than introducing parallel backends.
